seeding_documents: Nigerian jollof rice is famously prepared with a smoky party flavor, often cooked over open wood fires.
Danfo buses are the yellow commercial buses that dominate Lagos roads, known for aggressive driving and loud conductors.
NEPA and PHCN refer to Nigeria's power companies; 'UP NEPA!' is a joyful cry when electricity returns after an outage.
Suya is spicy grilled meat sold by mallams on street corners, typically beef or chicken with yaji spice.
Nollywood is the second largest film industry by volume, producing thousands of movies annually across Nigeria.
Owambe parties are lavish Yoruba celebrations with Aso Ebi uniforms, live bands, and abundant jollof rice.
Third Mainland Bridge connects Lagos Island to the mainland and is infamous for heavy traffic during rush hour.
Fuel scarcity is a recurring issue in Nigeria, leading to long queues at petrol stations and generator dependence.
Burna Boy and Wizkid are Grammy-recognized Nigerian artists who have brought Afrobeats to global audiences.
Bole (roasted plantain) and fish is a popular street food in Port Harcourt, usually served with spicy pepper sauce.
